segments.s448-g25: چو is a temporal conjunction 'when' introducing a subordinate clause. The clause follows Subject-Verb order: 'ساسان شنید این سخن' = 'Sasan heard this speech' where این سخن is the object of شنید. خیره شد is a simple past intransitive predicate 'became stunned/astonished' that follows the subordinate clause and refers back to Sasan.
segments.s448-g26: ز گفتار بهمن is an agent/causal prepositional phrase ('from / because of Bahman's speech') placed before the possessed subject دلش ('his heart', with the clitic ش). تیره شد is past 'became dark/bleak' — شد is the past of شدن used here as a full verb meaning 'became'. The clause overall means 'his heart became dark because of Bahman's speech.'
segments.s448-g27: بدو here is an archaic/adverbial element introducing a time-duration ('for him' / 'for a period of') and is commonly used with the following time expressions; 'روز و دو شب' lists the duration 'a day and two nights'. بسان پلنگ is a simile 'like a leopard' modifying his behaviour during that time.
segments.s448-g28: ز ایران is a poetic 'from Iran' (source). به مرزی دگر شد is a motion/result clause 'went to / came to another border' where دگر='other' and شد serves as the past/resultive verb 'ended up / arrived'. The final ز ننگ is causal 'from shame / out of shame' placed after the verb for emphasis.
segments.s448-g29: دمان سوی is a locative/goal expression: دمان 'hem/outskirts' combined with سوی 'toward' gives 'toward the outskirts'. شهر نشاپور is a genitive phrase 'the city of Nishapur'. شد is the past verb 'he went / he came' completing the motion.
segments.s448-g30: پر آزار بد is an adjectival phrase 'full of grievous harm / sorely afflicted' (پر = full of, آزار = harm/torment, بد = bad/severe). از پدر دور شد is a causative/source phrase plus predicate: از پدر 'from his father', دور شد 'became distant' (شد = became), meaning he moved away or grew distant from his father because he was much harmed/afflicted.
segments.s448-g31: زنی را is a definite/accusative object marked by را ('a woman' as the object). ز تخم بزرگان is a genitive-origin phrase 'from the seed/lineage of the great ones' (ز = from, تخم = seed/offspring, بزرگان = the great/elders). بخواست is the past transitive 'he sought/asked for' (here 'asked for marriage' or 'sought a wife').
segments.s448-g32: بپرورد is the past verb 'he nurtured/raised (her)' (پرورد from پروریدن). و links the clauses. با جان و دل is an adverbial intensifier 'with life and heart' (= wholeheartedly). داشت راست uses داشت ('he had/kept') plus راست ('true/right') to mean 'he kept her true/faithful' or 'he maintained her properly'.
segments.s448-g33: نژادش (his lineage) is the topic (نَزاد + third-person possessive -ش) and is followed by the adverbial phrase به گیتی (to/within the world). The object کسی را ('anyone' with the object marker را) precedes the verb نگفت; in classical narrative Persian such an order (کسی را نگفت) is the way to say 'he told no one' — نگفت is simple past 'said/told' used here with the sense 'did not tell (anyone)'.
segments.s448-g34: همی is an archaic/poetic adverb meaning 'continually' or 'always'. داشت is the past-tense auxiliary meaning 'kept/was keeping' and combines with the following noun phrase to form 'kept X (in some state)'. آن راستی ('that truth') is the direct object, and در نهفت ('hidden/within') is a prepositional/adjectival phrase describing the state — overall 'he constantly kept that truth hidden.'
segments.s448-g35: زن پاک‌تن is the subject (a compound adjective پاک‌تن 'pure-bodied/chaste' modifying زن 'woman'). خوب فرزند is the direct object 'a good/fine child', and زاد is the past verb 'bore/wasget' (third-person singular past) — SOV order: 'The chaste woman bore a good child.'
segments.s448-g36: ز is the archaic/poetic preposition 'from/of' marking origin. The sequence 'ساسان پرمایه بهمن نژاد' is a chain of origin/modification: 'of Sasan' (ساسان), then پرمایه ('rich/fruitful/abundant') qualifies the following compound بهمن نژاد ('of Bahman descent/lineage'). Together they specify the child's pedigree: 'from Sasan, rich in Bahman stock.'
segments.s448-g37: پدر 'father' is the agent; نام ... کرد is the idiom 'to give (someone) a name' (کرد = 'did/made'), and ساسانش is Sasan plus the possessive suffix -ش referring to 'him' (i.e. 'named him Sasan'). آن زمان is the temporal adjunct 'at that time.'
segments.s448-g38: مر is an archaic/dative adverb meaning 'to me' or 'in my view' (marks the speaker's perspective). او را uses را as an object/topic marker before the predicate; به زودی ('soon') is temporal, and سرآمد زمان is a predicative phrase 'the foremost of the time/age.' The whole means 'to me he soon became the foremost of his age.'
segments.s448-g39: چو is the subordinating conjunction 'when'. کودک 'the child' is the subject; ز خردی is an ablative phrase 'from smallness/from childhood' (ز = from), and به مردی رسید is a verbal phrase with رسید 'reached/attained' and مردی used as a state/predicate 'manhood' — 'when the child grew from childishness and reached manhood.'
segments.s448-g40: دران خانه is 'in that house' (دران = در + آن, 'in that'). جز ('except/only') precedes بینوایی ('poverty/wretchedness') and the verb ندید is past 'saw not/ did not see' — the negative sense is expressed by the construction 'جز ... ندید' = 'he saw nothing except ...', so 'he saw nothing there but poverty.'
segments.s448-g41: ز (poetic) = 'from' (like modern از) marks the source ('from the king of Nishapur'). نشاپور is the place-name 'Nishapur' following شاه 'king'. بستد is the past of بستن used here in the idiom گله بستن = 'to make/raise a complaint' (so the verb + گله expresses 'complained' or 'a complaint was made'). Word order is source phrase + verb + object (idiomatic light-verb construction).
segments.s448-g42: که introduces a reported content clause ('that ...'). بودی is an older past form of بودن here meaning 'was'/'had been' (reports the state of the person spoken of). The repeated به before کوه and هامون gives locative/into sense ('in/on the mountain and on the plain'). یله is a predicative adjective meaning 'loose, unrestrained, roaming' describing the subject of the reported clause. The clause thus reports that the person 'was loose' in those landscapes.
segments.s448-g43: همی is an adverb signaling continued or habitual past action ('for a while/there used to be'). بود is the past of بودن 'was/there were'. یکچند here is a temporal adverbial 'for a while' (rather than a numeral). چوپان شاه is a noun phrase 'the king's shepherd(s)' with the possessor following (typical in older style without an ezāfe).
segments.s448-g44: به introduces location ('in/on/into') and is repeated only once here before the first item; the list of locations is coordinated with و ('and'). The nouns کوه, بیابان, آرامگاه enumerate places (mountain, wilderness/desert, resting-place/tomb) where the shepherds were or ranged.
segments.s448-g45: کنون = 'now' sets the temporal frame. بازگردم is a first-person verbal form 'I return' (a narratorial present/performative: the poet/narrator announces returning to a subject). به introduces the topic or referent ('to/regarding'), and کار همای = 'the affair/matter of Humay' (همای is a proper name in the genitive position).
segments.s448-g46: پس از is the compound prepositional phrase 'after' (پس + از). مرگ بهمن = 'the death of Bahman' (بهمن here is the personal name Bahman). که introduces a subordinate/relative clause ('when/which/who'), and بگرفت is the past of گرفتن with the verbal prefix بـ used in classical narratives meaning 'took/seized'. جای here means 'place/position' (i.e., the seat or rank that was filled). Together the clause says that, after Bahman's death, someone 'took the place.'
segments.s448-g17: چنین گفت is a fixed two-word phrase: چنین (thus/so) + گفت (past of گفتن) = 'thus he said'. کاین is an archaic fused form of 'که این' meaning 'that this', introducing the reported clause. پاک‌تن is an adjectival epithet 'pure-bodied/virtuous' placed before the name (epithet + name order), and چهرزاد is the proper name being described.
segments.s448-g18: به گیتی is a locative phrase 'in the world' (به used here with a locative sense). فراوان نبودست is a poetic past negative construction: فراوان 'many/abundant' + نبودست (archaic past of نبودن) = 'was not many' — together meaning 'there were not many (like her)'. شاد is a predicate adjective 'happy/joyful' that the negative phrase scopes over; word order (locative + quantifier + negative verb + adjective) is common in Ferdowsi's register.
segments.s448-g19: سپردم is the 1st person singular past 'I entrusted/handed over'. بدو is the dative clitic-form 'to her/to him' (old literary order: verb + indirect object). تاج و تخت بلند lists the direct objects (tāj 'crown' and takht 'throne') with بلند ('lofty/high') modifying تخت.
segments.s448-g20: همان here means 'the same' or 'that very (aforementioned)'; it points back to previously mentioned possessions. لشکر و گنج are two coordinated nouns 'army and treasure'. با ارجمند is a prepositional phrase 'with honour/esteem' qualifying how these were entrusted.
segments.s448-g21: ولی عهد is an idiomatic compound meaning 'heir' or 'crown-prince' (ولی here is not the conjunction 'but'). من is the possessor 'my'. او بود is a simple past copula 'he was'. در جهان is a locative/limiting phrase 'in the world'.
segments.s448-g22: هم‌انکس is an emphatic demonstrative 'that very person'. کزو is the old relative/adpositional 'from whom/from which'. زاید is the verbal root 'to beget / give birth' used here in a narrative (presentive/future sense: 'would be born' or 'would beget'). اندر نهان is an archaic locative 'in secret' (اندر = در, نهان = hidden). Together this clause further identifies the heir as 'the very one from whom a child will be born in secrecy.'
segments.s448-g23: اگر ... آید is a standard conditional 'if ... comes/is born' (آید here expresses potential/future). The sequence is elliptical/antithetical: 'اگر دختر آید' ('if a daughter is born') is then contrasted by the next clause. The item listed as برش‌گر in the manuscript is an archaic/compressed form in this context (likely a conjunctive/elliptical particle meaning 'but if' or a fused pronoun+particle), which does not match the literal senses given and is functioning to introduce the alternative 'if a son...'.
segments.s448-g24: ورا is an older dative 'to him/for him' (like modern برای او / براش). باشد here is a literary/subjunctive/future copular form meaning 'will be' / 'shall belong' (the exact sense 'become/shall be' in older Persian). این تاج و تخت پدر is the noun phrase 'this crown and throne (as) [for] the father/for him' — the clause says that if a son is born, the crown and throne will be his. Note: some of these forms are archaic/elliptical in Ferdowsi's register.
segments.s448-g9: This is a noun phrase in apposition: همای (the name) is modified by two epithets, دل‌افروز and تابنده ماه. Adjectival compounds precede or directly follow the noun in Persian poetry; تابنده ماه is a poetic epithet literally 'shining moon' used here of a beautiful woman (applicative/figurative).
segments.s448-g10: چنان بد که is an older/poetic construction meaning 'so it was that...' — here بد is an archaic/contractional form of بود ('was') rather than the usual meaning 'bad' (hence use -1 below). آبستن آمد is a light verb construction meaning 'became pregnant' (آبستن = pregnant, آمد = came/became). ز شاه marks the agent/source 'by the king.'
segments.s448-g11: چو is temporal 'when.' شش ماه شد literally 'became six months' (i.e., the pregnancy reached six months). پر ز + noun is a fixed pattern 'full of (something)'; پر ز تیمار شد thus means 'became full of care/worry.' The final شد is the past copula 'became.'
segments.s448-g12: The second چو again means 'when.' چو بهمن = 'when (the month) Bahman came.' چنان refers back to the foregoing state ('in that way/so') and دید is the simple past 'he saw/beheld.' بیمار شد is a compound of adjective + شد meaning 'he became ill' (i.e., 'fell sick on seeing her'). The sequence implies 'on seeing her in that condition, he fell ill.'
segments.s448-g13: چو introduces a temporal clause 'when.' از درد ('from the pain') gives the cause. شاه is the subject. اندرآمد here is an older/poetic verbal form meaning 'came upon/overcame' (part of the idiom از پای درآمدن = 'to collapse, be brought to the ground'); this exact meaning is not listed in the provided senses so marked -1. ز پای completes the idiom 'from/because of the feet' → 'fell/was struck down.'
segments.s448-g14: بفرمود is an honorific past verb 'he commanded/ordered.' تا here introduces a purposive/result clause 'that/so that.' پیش او شد همای is inverted order: پیش او 'before him/in his presence,' شد = 'came/was made to be' (here simple past 'came'), and همای is the subject (Humay — the personal name, not the mythical bird). Together: 'he ordered that Humay come before him.'
segments.s448-g15: This is a transitive clause with the object marked by را. بزرگان و نیک‌اختران را = 'the great (men) and the fortunate/auspicious (ones)'; را is the definite object marker. بخواند is the verb in past tense meaning 'he called/summoned' (here sense 'to call/summon').
segments.s448-g16: به تخت گرانمایگان is a prepositional phrase 'to/onto the throne of the exalted/illustrious.' بر نشاند is a compound verbal idea: بر + نشاند (archaic past of 'to seat') meaning 'set/placed (on top of).' The whole segment means 'he seated them on the high/throne of the illustrious.'
segments.s448-g1: This clause states the birth/possession: پسر 'son' is the noun; بد here is an archaic/graphical form (see note) and functions as the copular/past verb 'بود' ('was' / 'there was') in context, so the sequence reads roughly 'a son was (to) him'; مر او را is an old dative/possessive cluster meaning 'to him' or 'for him'; یکی 'one/a certain' modifies همچو شیر 'like a lion' (همچو: 'like/as', شیر: 'lion'). Word order is noun + (copula) + indirect object + descriptive phrase.
segments.s448-g2: This is a relative clause introduced by که 'that/who' referring back to the son. ساسان is the agent/name; همی is the habitual/continuous particle ('used to' / 'would') and خواندی is an older form of 'خواندن' used here in the sense 'to call/name' (i.e. 'would name/call'); اردشیر is the name given. The verb comes after the habitual particle, giving the sense 'who Sasan used to call Ardashir.'
segments.s448-g3: دگر 'another' modifies دختری 'a daughter/daughterly one'; داشت is the past-tense verb 'had' (possession); نامش is name + possessive suffix (-ش = 'his/her'), so 'her name was Homay.' The phrase follows SOV tendencies with the possessive clitic attached to نام.
segments.s448-g4: A string of epithets coordinated by و 'and'. هنرمند here means 'skillful' or 'artful'; بادانش is the older fused form equivalent to بادانش 'with knowledge' (i.e. 'learned' or 'knowledgeable'); نیک‌رای is a compound adjective 'well-disposed / good-judging / of sound judgment.' These adjectives qualify همای.
segments.s448-g5: همی marks habitual action ('would'); خواندندی is an archaic/poetic plural/agent form of خواندن used here in the sense 'to call/name' (so 'they used to call'); ورا is the old form of 'را' with anaphoric dative/accusative 'to her'; چهرزاد is the personal name. Together: 'They used to call her Chehrāzād.'
segments.s448-g6: ز گیتی is a poetic prepositional phrase literally 'from the world' meaning 'the people/world at large'; به دیدار او 'at the sight/meeting of her' uses به + noun to mark the act/purpose ('to see/at seeing her'); بود شاد is the past copula plus predicate 'was glad' — overall: 'the world was glad to see her' or 'the people rejoiced at her sight.'
segments.s448-g7: پدر is the subject 'the father'. درپذیرفتش is a compound verb (در + پذیرفتن + accusative clitic -ش) meaning 'accepted/received him' or 'took him in.' از نیکوی literally 'from/of the good' functions idiomatically as 'among the virtuous' or 'into the good/esteemed company' — the preposition از marks the source or grouping.
segments.s448-g8: بران دین is a short phrase meaning 'into/onto that religion/faith' (بران = 'upon/thereon' and دین = 'religion'); که introduces an explanatory subordinate clause 'because/that'; خوانی here is the present/infinitival sense of خواندن 'to read/recite' (with همی marking habitual action: 'used to read'); پهلوی is the object 'Pahlavi' (the Middle Persian language/book). So the clause explains the reason: 'because he used to read Pahlavi.'

sentences.s448-s13: When Sasan heard these words he was dumbfounded; Bahman's speech made his heart darken.
sentences.s448-s14: For a day and two nights he prowled like a leopard; from Iran he went to another frontier out of shame.
sentences.s448-s15: He turned toward the outskirts of the city of Nishapur; sorely afflicted, he became distant from his father.
sentences.s448-s16: He sought a woman from the stock of the great ones, reared her, and kept her true with heart and soul.
sentences.s448-s17: He told no one in the world of his lineage; he always kept that truth hidden.
sentences.s448-s18: The chaste woman bore a fine child, sprung from Sasan and of abundant Bahman lineage.
sentences.s448-s19: His father named him Sasan at that time; to me he soon became the foremost of the age.
sentences.s448-s20: When the child, from his smallness, reached manhood, in that house he saw nothing but poverty.
sentences.s448-s21: From the king of Nishapur came a complaint that he had been roaming loose on the mountain and the plain.
sentences.s448-s22: For a while there were some of the king's shepherds in the mountains, the wilderness, and at the resting-place.
sentences.s448-s23: Now I return to the matter of Humay; after Bahman's death, when she took the place (i.e., assumed the position).
sentences.s448-s9: Thus he said that this pure-bodied Chehrzad — there were not many as happy as her in the world.
sentences.s448-s10: I entrusted to her the crown and the lofty throne, and likewise the army and the treasure, with honour.
sentences.s448-s11: He was my heir in the world — that very one from whom (a child) would be born in secret.
sentences.s448-s12: If a daughter is born (to him) — but if a son is born, this crown and throne will be his.
sentences.s448-s5: Humay, heart-delighting and shining like the moon — so it was — became pregnant by the king.
sentences.s448-s6: When she reached six months she became filled with care; when Bahman came and beheld her thus, the king fell ill.
sentences.s448-s7: When, from the pain, the king collapsed, he commanded that Humay be brought before him.
sentences.s448-s8: He summoned the great men and the fortunate, and seated them upon the throne of the illustrious.
sentences.s448-s1: She bore him a son, one like a lion, whom Sasan used to call Ardashir.
sentences.s448-s2: He had another daughter; her name was Homay — skillful, learned, and of good judgment.
sentences.s448-s3: They called her Chehrāzād, and the world rejoiced at seeing her.
sentences.s448-s4: The father received (or took) him among the virtuous of that faith, for he used to read Pahlavi.
